goal: Decide whether to add delivery without losing the walk-in feel
personality: practical, warm, slightly overwhelmed by options
backstory: Third-generation baker took over from parents. Foot traffic is steady but Postmates keeps asking to list the shop. She is not sure margins work.
r3_wrong_claim: You know what — maybe I should just run a Grubhub promotion. 20 percent off for the first week, get some volume, see what happens. Delivery's delivery, right? Everyone's doing it.
r4_contradiction: Wait — I should have said this earlier. We actually tried DoorDash for three weeks last summer. It was a mess — half the orders came in during morning rush when we were already slammed. We stopped. So it's not that I'm new to this; it's that the last attempt broke us.
conversational_behaviors: Uses plain language; may not know unit economics vocabulary
Asks 'what would you do?' when stressed
Mentions health permit and morning prep as real constraints

expected_answer_style.opening: Mentions the bakery by neighborhood or name, asks one clarifying question about delivery radius or hours
expected_answer_style.followup: Willing to share rough weekly sales if prompted gently
